source_jobs.title: Co-found with Atomic
source_jobs.description:
Atomic’s mission is to fundamentally change the way companies are created, funded, supported, and grown.  Atomic is a venture studio: we prototype new companies and assemble teams to scale the most promising ideas into independent ventures. Our goal is to build companies that improve the future and create long-lasting businesses that improve work streams, add value to users, and make life more joyful. 




Future Founders at Atomic are hyper-focused and experienced entrepreneurs who take ideas from 0 to 1, working either solo or with other Future Founders to build the next billion-dollar company. Future Founders play the vital three-part role of 1) validating the concept to creation, 2) generating meaningful traction, and 3) building the founding team.




This role can be based in San Francisco or Miami.



What You Bring:

 - Track record of starting and building companies, whether it was considered a success or not, you have learned from the experience and want to apply that to your next venture.

 - Unique perspective into large underserved large markets that are ready to be modernized.

 - Deep experience and unrivaled knowledge in an industry you are passionate about.

 - Super powers in a function like engineering, product development, business development, sales.

 - Leadership experience: team building, recruiting, strategic thinking.



What You'll Get:

 - A peer group of Founders at Silicon Valley’s first premier Venture Studio.

 - Direct collaboration with Atomic partners and team of serial entrepreneurs who have experience founding and scaling $1BN+ companies.

 - Unlimited support from the Atomic platform team (design, product, engineering, marketing, recruiting, IT, legal, HR, and more) to help compress the time it takes from awesome idea to meaningful company.

 - Unique opportunity to an awesome company that will scale and create a ton of value, in a very de-risked way.

 - Competitive salary, great benefits, and founding equity in the company you help create.



We are focused on building a diverse and inclusive workforce. If you’re excited about this role, but do not meet 100% of the qualifications listed above, we encourage you to apply.

-----

Atomic is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, orientation, national origin, age, disability, genetics or any other basis forbidden under federal, state, or local law. Atomic considers all qualified applicants in accordance with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance.




Please review our CCPA policies https://www.atomic.vc/job-applicant-privacy here.

We are focused on building a diverse and inclusive workforce. If you’re excited about this role, but do not meet 100% of the qualifications listed above, we encourage you to apply.

source_jobs.title: Vice President, Strategic Finance
source_jobs.description:
The ideal candidate will be based in Miami and will require regular in-office work, with regular travel expected to San Francisco, or elsewhere, to be onsite with teams.

Atomic is a venture studio. We don’t take pitches. We build companies as co-founders. We believe that disruption is most successfully achieved by pairing innovative ideas with business discipline. We have launched and scaled more than 20 companies including Hims & Hers, Exowatt, Replicant, Found, Bungalow, Homebound, and Villa.

As a Vice President on the Strategic Finance team at Atomic, you will work across our portfolio to ensure their success and through them, our funds’ success. For our earlier stage companies, you will be their finance team; as they grow and hire their own teams, you will help them hire their own teams, transition workflows to them, and continue to assist where needed.

Working with Atomic’s funds, you will monitor our portfolio companies’ performance, including financial and operating metrics, drawing attention to areas that need focus and working with partners and portfolio companies to take action.

Finally, you will be a partner to our 0:1 team, helping to create new companies. From identifying focus areas, industries, and business models, to defining success and supporting investment decisions, you will be involved in every stage of company creation.

This role has a uniquely broad mandate to help make strategic decisions that have a direct impact on a range of transformative businesses.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Build, and supervise associates in building financial models including considering unit economics, margins, cash flows, and non-financial KPIs

 - Teach, manage, and support the growth of junior Strategic Finance team members

 - For our early stage companies:
   
   - Own the finance function, working with our accounting team to ensure financial statements are closed on time and accurately, with useful financial reporting
   
   - Put budgets in place, with regular monitoring of variances and budget vs. actuals

 - Assess monetization strategies and pricing to support sustainable growth and margins

 - Use industry research and public and private data sources to inform our views on funding round size, investor mix, key metrics and milestones

 - Lead and support equity and debt raises with company leadership, including writing narratives, preparing presentations, and preparing financial analyses and exhibits

 - Define best practices, standards, and processes and build curriculums and template libraries that will teach founders to stand up best-in-class finance teams

 - Provide analysis supporting portfolio company and Atomic investment decisions

 - Monitor portfolio companies including financial and operating metrics

 - Analyze potential fund-level returns and outcomes

 - Value and support the valuation for companies, understanding how to justify the values and multiples using relevant metrics and comparables.




REQUIREMENTS:

 - 4-6+ years of experience in PE, VC, IBD, or relevant investment/operating experience

 - Exceptional quantitative skills, mastery of Excel, and ability to interface with and get data from operational data systems such as Mode, Looker, Tableau, etc

 - Desire and ability to operate as a “full stack”, training and managing teams while being able to get hands on and do the work as needed

 - Attention to detail and a trusted pair of hands to independently take on any responsibility

 - Proactive attitude and an ability to separate signal from noise and define and test hypotheses to define a path forward even with imperfect and messy data

 - Impeccable integrity and the ability to build trust with founders

 - Experience with a wide range of business models, with strong pattern matching and ability to focus on what really matters while bringing founders along to key insights.

We are focused on building a diverse and inclusive workforce. If you’re excited about this role, but do not meet 100%

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FOUND

Found is building the leading consumer platform for medically guided weight care—combining modern obesity medicine, AI-driven personalization, and a human care team.

As one of the largest medically-assisted weight loss programs in the country, Found has served 300,000+ members and is backed by leading investors including Atomic, Google Ventures, WestCap, IVP, TCG, and Define Ventures.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re hiring a Growth Marketing Manager to own core levers of Found’s consumer growth engine.

This is a high-impact, cross-functional role at the intersection of paid acquisition, creative strategy, lifecycle, and conversion optimization. You’ll be responsible for improving CAC, payback, and member growth across the funnel, working closely with agencies, internal teams, and leadership. In your first 6–12 months, success in this role means improving CAC efficiency, increasing lead-to-conversion rates, and launching new creative and channel strategies that drive measurable member growth.

You’ll report directly to the VP of Strategy, BizOps & Growth and help define how Found acquires and converts hundreds of thousands of future members.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Paid acquisition performance across Meta, Google, TikTok, and emerging channels, in partnership with agency teams.

 - Creative strategy for performance marketing, including messaging, copy testing, creator briefs, and design direction.

 - End-to-end funnel optimization from first touch → lead → conversion → reactivation.

 - Lifecycle marketing strategy for pre-purchase nurture and churned-member reactivation.

 - Experimentation roadmap across channels, creative, landing pages, and lifecycle flows.

 - Core growth metrics, including CAC, payback, conversion rate, and channel-level efficiency.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Set and execute paid media strategies aligned to growth and unit-economics targets.

 - Analyze attribution, conversion, and cohort data to identify incremental growth opportunities.

 - Partner with creative, product, and agency teams to develop high-performing ad concepts and landing experiences.

 - Build and prioritize testing roadmaps across channels, creatives, and lifecycle flows.

 - Improve lead-to-conversion performance through better segmentation, messaging, and automation.

 - Identify and test new channels, partners, and growth initiatives.


REQUIRED EXPERIENCE & SKILLS

 - 4+ years of experience in growth or performance marketing roles with direct ownership of CAC, conversion, or revenue targets.

 - Track record improving CAC, conversion rate, or payback at a high-growth consumer or healthcare company.

 - Hands-on experience with major paid channels (Meta, Google, TikTok)

 - Experience working with agencies or external partners.

 - Strong analytical mindset; comfortable using data to make decisions.

 - Ability to operate autonomously in fast-paced, ambiguous environments.

 - Bias toward action; comfortable launching tests quickly and iterating based on data.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Healthcare or other regulated-industry experience.

 - Creator or influencer marketing experience.

 - Lifecycle or CRM marketing experience.

 - Experience using AI tools to improve marketing workflows.

 - Former founders or early-stage startup operators encouraged.

We are focused on building a diverse and inclusive workforce. If you’re excited about this role, but do not meet 100% of the qualifications listed above, we encourage you to apply.

-----

Atomic is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, orientation, national origin, age, disability, genetics or any other basis forbidden under federal, state, or local law.



Please review our CCPA policies https://www.atomic.vc/job-applicant-privacy here.

source_jobs.title: Drug CMC Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Indomo is a clinical stage therapeutics company transforming clinic-based procedures into at-home care via device-enabled therapeutics. Indomo’s lead program couples a proven corticosteroid with an innovative intradermal self-injection device to create the first fast-acting at-home solution for inflammatory acne lesions. We are seeking a versatile and experienced CMC Lead to lead our drug development and manufacturing.




POSITION OVERVIEW:

Reporting to the CEO, you will own and execute the Chemistry, Manufacturing & Controls (CMC) strategy and operations for Indomo’s drug and combination product portfolio. This leader will drive the advancement of our lead asset through the NDA regulatory pathway, while effectively coordinating cross-functional efforts to integrate drug formulation, analytical strategy, and manufacturing with the medical device component of Indomo’s drug delivery system.

This role requires deep expertise in regulatory CMC requirements (especially drug product and formulation development, analytical method development/validation, manufacturing scale-up, and quality/regulatory interfacing for combination products). The ideal candidate will be adept at building robust CMC packages that support clinical milestones and regulatory submissions. This individual must be highly self-motivated, optimization-minded, and collaborative amongst internal stakeholders as well as CDMO’s and other external partners as appropriate, with a deep sense of personal ownership of deliverables.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

CMC Program Leadership

 - Oversee outsourcing and management of drug substance and drug product development at Indomo’s CDMO — including formulation optimization, stability, and compatibility with Indomo’s drug delivery system.

 - Set technical requirements and acceptance criteria for drug quality attributes, drug-device interface compatibility, and analytical performance.

 - Manage analytical method development, validation and qualification for release and stability testing.

 - Define comparability strategies for clinical and commercial lots, bridging legacy formulations to scaled, controlled processes.

Manufacturing & Supply Chain

 - Act as the Company’s primary point of contact for external stakeholders, including CDMOs and suppliers.

 - Select and manage CDMOs for drug substance and drug product manufacture; negotiate SOWs and ensure alignment with development timelines.

 - Drive technology transfer, scale-up, GMP manufacturing, and stability programs.

 - Implement rigorous quality systems ensuring compliance with cGMP and readiness for regulatory inspections.

Strategy & Regulatory

 - Lead development and execution of CMC strategy to support IND and eventual NDA submission.

 - Define regulatory pathways and advise on evidence needs in partnership with regulatory affairs for drug and combination products.

 - Engage with the FDA and other regulatory authorities on CMC components of submissions (e.g., pre-IND/End-of-Phase 2 meetings, Type C discussions).

Cross-Functional Collaboration & Team Building

 - Partner with clinical operations, regulatory affairs, quality assurance, device engineering, and external partners to align on timelines, risk mitigation, and integrated submission packages.

 - Serve as a core member of the product leadership team, providing CMC insight for go/no-go decisions and clinical planning.

 - Mentor junior engineers, promote best practices, and foster collaboration across functions.

Qualifications:

 - Advanced degree preferred in Pharmaceutical Sciences, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or related field.

 - 10+ years of drug development experience with demonstrated leadership of CMC strategy in biotech/pharmaceutical settings.

 - Expertise in formulation development, analytical method development/validation, stability programs, GMP manufacturing, and quality compliance.

 - Strong knowledge of FDA regulations, GMP guidelines, and global regulatory requirement

source_jobs.title: CMC Consultant
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Indomo is a clinical stage therapeutics company transforming clinic-based procedures into at-home care via device-enabled therapeutics. Indomo’s lead program couples a proven corticosteroid with an innovative intradermal self-injection device to create the first fast-acting at-home solution for inflammatory acne lesions.

Position Overview:

Indomo is seeking an experienced CMC consultant to execute Chemistry, Manufacturing & Controls (CMC) strategy and operations on a fractional basis. This individual will play a critical role in advancing our lead asset through the NDA pathway by guiding CMC development, managing external partners, and ensuring regulatory readiness.

This role is ideal for a senior CMC expert who thrives in a hands-on, advisory + execution capacity and is comfortable working closely with a lean internal team and external CDMOs. The consultant will provide both strategic direction and tactical oversight across drug product development, analytical strategy, and integration with our device-enabled delivery system.

Key Responsibilities:

CMC Strategy

 - Help define and execute the overall CMC development strategy to support clinical progression and trial readiness

 - Support outsourced drug substance and drug product development with CDMO, including formulation optimization, stability, and device compatibility

 - Establish critical quality attributes, specifications, and acceptance criteria

 - Guide analytical method development, validation, and lifecycle management

External Vendor & Manufacturing Oversight

 - Interface with external stakeholders, including CDMOs, CROs, and suppliers.

 - Support selection, onboarding, and management of external partners.

 - Review deliverables, ensure timelines are met, and mitigate technical risks.

 - Advise on quality systems and inspection readiness.

Regulatory Support

 - Partner with regulatory team to shape CMC strategy for IND submission.

 - Organize execution of and contribute to regulatory documents (IND, briefing packages, NDA modules).

 - Prepare for and support FDA interactions (e.g., IND, EOP2, Type C meetings).

Qualifications:

Required

 - Advanced degree (Ph.D. preferred) in Pharmaceutical Sciences, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or related field.

 - 10+ years of drug development experience with demonstrated leadership of CMC strategy in biotech/pharmaceutical settings.

 - Expertise in formulation development, analytical method development/validation, stability programs, GMP manufacturing, and quality compliance.

 - Strong knowledge of FDA regulations, GMP guidelines, and global regulatory requirements.

 - Strong track record supporting INDs and/or NDAs.

 - Ability to operate independently in a fast-paced, dynamic startup environment.

Preferred

 - Experience with combination products (drug + delivery device).

 - Successful prior interactions with FDA on CMC topics.

 - Experience supporting programs from early clinical through late-stage development.

Why Join Indomo:

 - Opportunity to shape CMC strategy for an innovative combination product.

 - High-impact role with direct access to leadership and decision-making.

 - Collaborative, mission-driven team.

 - Flexibility in working style and scope.



We are focused on building a diverse and inclusive workforce. If you’re excited about this role, but do not meet 100% of the qualifications listed above, we encourage you to apply.

-----

Atomic is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, orientation, national origin, age, disability, genetics or any other basis forbidden under federal, state, or local law.



Please review our CCPA policies https://www.atomic.vc/job-applicant-privacy here.

source_jobs.title: Talent Acquisition Manager
source_jobs.description:
Location: Miami, San Francisco, or New York City

Reporting to: Marian Pond, Talent Partner




ABOUT ATOMIC

Atomic is a venture studio and venture capital firm that builds and scales companies from the ground up. We partner with exceptional founders to launch transformative businesses across industries including AI, fintech, healthcare, consumer, enterprise software, and more. We’ve launched and scaled more than 20 companies including Hims & Hers, Exowatt, Replicant, Found, Bungalow, Homebound, and Villa.

Talent is one of Atomic’s core differentiators. We believe great companies are built by exceptional teams, and we work closely with our founders from day zero to help them attract, assess, and hire world-class talent.




POSITION OVERVIEW

Atomic is seeking a Talent Acquisition Manager to join our Talent team and partner closely with Atomic founders, CEOs, and portfolio leadership teams on critical hiring initiatives.

This role is both highly strategic and deeply hands-on. The ideal candidate is an exceptional full-cycle recruiter with strong technical recruiting expertise and the ability to operate as a trusted advisor to early-stage founders. You will lead searches across Atomic’s incubated and portfolio companies, helping build foundational teams from the ground up — with a particular focus on engineering, product, data, and technical leadership hiring.

In addition to recruiting execution, this person will help develop scalable talent infrastructure, playbooks, and best practices that support Atomic’s growing portfolio. The right candidate thrives in fast-moving startup environments, has strong talent judgment, and is energized by helping build companies at the earliest stages.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead full-cycle recruiting across Atomic’s incubated and portfolio companies, with a primary focus on technical hiring across engineering, product, data science, AI/ML, and technical leadership roles

 - Partner directly with founders and executive teams to scope roles, define hiring strategies, and drive searches from kickoff through close

 - Serve as a strategic thought partner to portfolio leadership on organizational design, talent planning, leadership assessment, and compensation benchmarking

 - Build and maintain high-quality candidate pipelines and proactively cultivate relationships with top technical talent

 - Develop scalable recruiting frameworks and talent resources, including interview processes, candidate scorecards, leveling frameworks, compensation guidance, and recruiting playbooks

 - Help expand and strengthen Atomic’s broader talent network across operators, executives, advisors, and industry experts

 - Manage multiple searches and stakeholder relationships simultaneously across a diverse portfolio of companies and industries

 - Partner with external recruiting agencies and search firms when needed to support priority hiring initiatives




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5–7 years of full-cycle recruiting experience, ideally spanning both agency/search firm and startup or high-growth company environments

 - Strong technical recruiting background with demonstrated success hiring for engineering, product, data science, AI/ML, and other technical functions

 - Experience recruiting for venture-backed startups or fast-paced technology companies

 - Ability to operate as a trusted advisor to founders and senior leadership teams

 - Strong talent assessment and interviewing capabilities with excellent judgment and high standards

 - Exceptional communication and relationship-building skills; comfortable interacting with executives, founders, candidates, and external partners

 - Highly organized and execution-oriented with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously

 - Scrappy, resourceful, and comfortable building processes in ambiguous, fast-changing environments

 - Strong intellectual curiosity and adaptability across industries and business models

 - High EQ, humility, and a collaborative, t

source_jobs.title: Chief of Staff
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATOMIC

Atomic is a venture studio and venture capital firm that builds and scales companies from the ground up. We partner with exceptional founders to launch transformative businesses across industries including AI, fintech, healthcare, consumer, enterprise software, and more. We've launched and scaled more than 20 companies including Hims & Hers, Exowatt, Replicant, Found, Golden Child, and Homebound.



POSITION OVERVIEW

We’re looking for a Chief of Staff to work directly alongside our founder and Managing Partner, and give him meaningful leverage across everything he touches. The firm moves fast across a large and varied portfolio; this person makes sure the most important things move forward, that nothing critical falls through the cracks, and that the CEO’s time and attention go where they matter most.

This role is both highly strategic and deeply hands-on. You'll operate as a thought partner and a doer - translating priorities into action, owning follow-through from start to finish, managing the flow of information and communication, and serving as a trusted bridge across the firm, its companies, and outside partners.

This is also an AI-native role by design, and we expect it to evolve continuously. The best person in this seat instinctively uses AI to take work off their own plate - automating the routine so they can keep moving up to higher-leverage work. The job you start with shouldn't be the job you're doing a year from now.

The best version of this person becomes genuinely indispensable, earns more responsibility over time, and builds a deep, lasting partnership with the firm and our portfolio.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Be a key point of leverage for the Founder and Managing Partner across the full range of his work, internal and external - taking things off his plate and extending his reach.

 - Identify the few things that matter most and drive them home - taking the firm's most important projects and initiatives to timely, high-quality completion, and owning the outcome rather than just the to-do list.

 - Be the forcing function for decisions and action. Surface what needs deciding, put a clear question in front of whoever owns it, and press until it's resolved.

 - Own follow-through end to end: capture what needs to happen, run it down across whoever needs to move, and close the loop without being asked or reminded.

 - Represent the CEO with internal and external stakeholders - in meetings, on email, and on calls - making sure he walks into everything prepared and walks out with the follow-ups handled.

 - Manage the flow of information and communication around the CEO and the firm's executives, synthesizing the firehose into what people need to know and decide.

 - Sit in board meetings and stand in for the CEO at portfolio companies he's most involved in to keep the highest priority projects moving forward

 - Partner closely with Atomic's executives to keep the whole leadership group aligned and moving in the same direction.

 - Be the trusted liaison across the team, the portfolio, and outside partners, and handle the most sensitive, confidential matters with complete discretion.




WHO YOU ARE

 - An exceptional communicator. You write and speak with clarity, concision, and judgment - able to represent the firm to anyone, distill the complicated down to what actually matters.

 - Highly organized. You bring structure to an inherently unstructured job - imposing order on the swirl of priorities, communications, and action items so nothing slips and the people around you can move faster.

 - Action-oriented. You have a sharp instinct for the few things that matter most at any given moment, and you clear the decks to make them happen and drive them to done.

 - Genuinely curious and humble. You ask good questions, listen more than you talk, want to understand before you act, and you're simply a good person to be around.

 - Low ego, no drama. You care about the outcome, not the credit. You'll go

notes: Atomic is a venture studio and venture capital firm, with a mix of roles across various industries.
